content_of_post_to_comment:
When life feels overwhelming, ambitious plans can create even more pressure. Sometimes progress begins with something very small and repeatable rather than a dramatic transformation.

It could be making the bed, taking a short walk, writing down one priority, contacting one person or completing ten minutes of focused work.

What small habit helped you begin moving forward again, and why do you think it worked when bigger plans did not?
comment: Making the bed or doing ten minutes of work sounds almost too simple, but I think small wins help because they rebuild trust with yourself. When bigger plans fail, it’s often because they depend on motivation staying high. A tiny habit gives you a way to act even on bad days, and that momentum can quietly change how you see yourself.
